def: adv. = ćau-khūṅṭ, q.v.s.v. ćau.

s.f. A square and low seat or pedestal, a frame to sit on, a stool, a bench, a chair (prop. kursī); a small throne; — a commode; — a low board; a kneading-board; — an ornament worn on the breast, a square pendant (of a chain, &c.), a brooch; a station (of police, of customs, of toll, or of the railway, &c.); a guard's post, an outpost; a party of peons, &c. stationed, a guard, a watch; watching; — a band (of musicians, &c.); — harlotry, prostitution; pay or hire of a courtezan; — an aggregate of four; — a measure of corn (equal to four ser)
subs: v.n. To relieve a guard; — a guard to be relieved; — ćaukī bharnā, To make an offering to a deity
s.m. Watch and ward; one's turn of watch or guard
adj. & s.m. Keeping watch or guard; — a watchman, guard, sentinel
s.f. The office or business of a watchman; the pay or hire of a watchman; the tax on account of watch and ward
To watch (for, -kī); to watch, guard, mount guard; to give a seat (to, -ko)
s.f. Evading the market tax; smuggling
(-ko), To keep (one) in the guard-house or in custody, to detain (a suspected person

def: v.n. = ćauṅknā, q.v.
adj. = ćau-koṇ, q.v.s.v. ćau.

ety: ćauk, q.v.+ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A gathering or assemblage of four (see ćaukṛī); — division of a crop in which the cultivator gives only a fourth.

ety: ćaukṛā+āt = āwat = Prk. वत्ता (with pleon. ā) = S. वार्त्ता
def: s.f. An assembly of four; (hence) a council of arbitration.
s.f. A measure of grain, the fourth of a ćauthiyā.

ety: ćauk, q.v.+ṛī = aṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. An aggregate of four; an assembly of four (persons); a carriage and four; a period comprising the four yugas or ages; — bound (as of a deer, &c. — the four legs being all drawn up at once), spring, bounce
subs: v.n. To bound along; to bound, spring, leap, bounce
v.n. To forget (one's) bound, to lose (one's) elastic tread; to be fascinated, be bewildered or confounded, to have (one's) senses benumbed

def: adv. On all four sides, all round.
adv. On all four sides, all round.
s.m. Tonsure, &c. (see what follows)
subs: s.m. The ceremony of tonsure; cutting off all the hair from the head of a child three years old, except one lock on the crown.

def: s.m. A kind of bean or pulse, Dolichos sinensis (syn. lobhiā, boṛā).
s.f. A species of pot-herb, Amaranthus polygamus (there are two kinds, one red, called lāl sāg or lāl ćaulāʼī; the other green, called also marsā, gindhārī, gander or harī ćaulāʼī); — wild rice.
s.m. The fourth reservoir to which water is raised by the beṛī and daurī for the purpose of irrigation.
adj. (prov.) = ćanćal, q.v.
s.m. = ćaunḍū, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Dazzled; dim-sighted, &c. ( = ćundhā, q.v.).
s.m. (?) — ćauṅdhī, q.v.
s.f. Darkness (through the eyes being dazzled), dimness, dulness of sight.

ety: ćauṅdhiyā = ćaundhī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To be dazzled, &c. ( = ćundhiyānā, q.v.); to be struck with astonishment or amazement, to be bewildered, be confused, to be frightened out of one's senses.
adj. (f. -ī) = ćundhlā, q.v.
v.n. = ćundhlānā, q.v.
s.m. = ćau-ḍol, q.v.
s.m. A blockhead, dolt, fool.
s.m. Wheedling, cajolery
s.f. Wheedling; sharp practice, &c. ( = ćauṅgā)
subs: s.m. A wheedler, cajoler; a sharper

def: s.f. The act of starting, a sudden start.
v.t. To cause to start, to startle; to rouse, to waken; to make vigilant, put on the alert
adj. & s.m. Boggling, timid, shy, wild; — an untamed animal; an animal that shies, a boggler.

ety: ćauṅk˚ = Prk. चमक्क(इ) or चमक्के(इ), or Ap. Prk. चवँक्क(इ) = S. चमत्+क्रियते pass. of कृ (used actively)
def: v.n. To be startled, to be roused (from sleep); to start, boggle, wince; to be vigilant, be on the alert
subs: ćauṅk-paṛnā, v.n. (intens.) To start up; to wake up suddenly; to bounce up; to wince, to boggle ( = ćauṅknā).

def: s.f. = ćaulāʼī, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. the caus. of S. rt. क्षुभ्
def: s.f. Incentive, stimulus; avidity, alacrity, wish, desire, fondness; — a gold ornament worn on the front teeth — ćauṅp dilwānā (-ko), To stimulate, to rouse, to spur on, to incite, to excite, to inflame.
s.m. = ćauṅṛā, q.v.
s.m. A well or subterranean apartment for grain.

s.f. A chowrie, the bushy tail of the Bos grunniens used as a fly-flap or fan (and as one of the insignia of royalty), a fly-flap, a whisk (made of a kind of grass, or of horse-hair, or of peacock's feathers).
s.f. A police station, the kotwal's office (cf. ćaurī, ćaubār, ćaukī).
s.m. = ćauṅḍū, q.v.
s.m. = ćausar, q.v.
adj. = ćausaṭh, q.v.
s.f. Cotton-pods, &c. in which the fibre is equal to one-fourth of the whole produce.
s.m. = ćautarā, ćabūtrā, q.v.
adj. Thirty-four.
adj. & s.m. = ćau-pāʼe, ćau-pāya, q.v.

ety: Prk. चउपाए or चउप्पाए; S. चतुष्+पादः
def: adj. & s.m. Four-footed; — a quadruped, a beast, an animal; — any four-footed piece of furniture.
s.m. = ćaupālā, q.v.; cf. also ćau-pahal, ćau-pahlū, s.v. ćau.

ety: Prk. चउपाइआ or चउप्पाइआ; S. चतुष्+पादिका
def: s.f. A kind of metre or verse consisting of four padas or short lines; — a singing party in the holī season
subs: s.m. A verse of four lines, each of fifteen instants.

def: s.f. = ćaupāʼī, q.v.
subs: s.m. A verse of four lines, each of thirty instants.

def: s.f. = ćaupāʼī, q.v.
subs: s.m. A verse of four lines, each of thirty instants.

def: adj. Quadrilateral; — s.m. = ćau-pāya.
s.m. = ćaubār, q.v.
s.m. A litter, a sedan.
adj. & s.m. = ćau-pāya; — s.m. = ćau-paṛ, q.v.

s.f. A game played with oblong dice (like ćausar); the cloth or board on which the game is played (having two transverse bars in the form of a cross); — the form of a cross; — ćaupaṛ-bāz, s.m. A ćaupaṛ-player
subs: adj. Having the form of a cross
ćaupaṛ-bāzār, s.m. A market-place in the form of a ćaupaṛ cloth, i.e. having two transverse roads passing through it.

def: s.m. = ćaubār, q.v.
adj. Open all round; wide open; — flat, levelled; razed, ruined, destroyed; — s.f. A desolate place, ruin, waste
subs: v.t. To level; to raze, to ruin, demolish, destroy.

ety: Prk. चउपाए or चउप्पाए; S. चतुष्+पादः
def: adj. & s.m. Four-footed; — a quadruped, a beast, an animal; — any four-footed piece of furniture.
s.m. A large open space in a forest; a large tract of low land ( = ćauṛā).
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi چور (cvr /⁠cor⁠/) (c. 1392), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭 (cora), from Sanskrit चोर (corá), चौर (caurá). Compare Romani chor.

def: adj. & s.m. = ćauṛā, q.v.
adj. Demolished, destroyed, ruined, spoiled; decayed; bad, vicious

adj. (f. -ī), Wide, broad, expanded; ample; pertaining to breadth, of breadth or width; — destroyed, ruined; abandoned (as a tenement, &c.); — s.m. A large open space in a jungle or remote from human habitation ( = ćaur)
subs: v.t. To make broad, to widen, to enlarge, &c. ( = ćauṛānā)
adv. In the open; in broad day
To be left desolate, to be abandoned, be quite forsaken; to be ruined
v.n. To be wide, &c.; to be ruined, be undone.

def: s.m. = ćaulā, q.v.
s.m. The place where, or the funeral pile on which, satī is performed; — also = ćautarā, ćabūtrā, q.v.

ety: ćauṛā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Breadth, width; latitude; extension, extent; expansion, dilatation; — boasting.

ety: ćauṛā, q.v.+ān = Prk. अअणं = अत्तणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.f.m. Breadth, width, &c. ( = ćauṛāʼī).

ety: ćauṛā+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To increase in breadth, to make broad, to widen, to enlarge, to expand; — to ruin, destroy, to spoil, plunder.
s.m. A practice of the sword exercise, cutting off the four legs (of an animal) at one blow; — a game formerly played during the four months of the rainy season
subs: (-ke), To cut off the four legs (of an animal) at one blow
(-ke), To hamstring (a beast); to lose the use of the limbs (by illness).

def: s.m. A species of metre.
adj. Ninety-four.
adj. = ćaurānwe, q.v.
s.m. = ćauṛāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Breadth, width, &c. ( = ćauṛāʼī).
s.m. A shed, &c. ( = ćaubār, q.v.).
adj. Level, even, plane; square; — adv. On all four sides, all round
subs: v.t. To make even, to level ( = ćaurasānā).

def: s.f. Equality of surface, evenness, levelness.

ety: ćauras, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To make even or smooth, to level.

adj. Eighty-four; — s.f. A subdivision (in former times) of a pargana or district comprising eighty-four villages; — small bells (such as are hung from the necks of draught oxen, or under the seat of a native vehicle); morris-bells (worn on the ankles by dancing girls); — the varieties or different stages of transmigration
subs: To undergo all the transmigrations in store for one; to be punished for one's sins
s.f. An epithet of the goddess Kālī.

def: s.f. Levelling, planing; — a granary above ground.
s.f. = ćauṅrī, and ćaubār, qq.v.
s.f. Thievery, theft, robbing; fraud.
s.m. Theft, &c. ( = ćorī, q.v.).
s.m. Powder; flower, meal.
s.m. Land four times ploughed (cf. ćausarī).

ety: Prk. चउस्सलिओ; S. चतुर+स्टतः, p.p.p. of rt. स्ट
def: s.m. (f.?) A game like paćīsī, but played with dice instead of cowries (cf. ćaupaṛ, from which it differs only in the form of the game); the cloth or board on which the game is played.
s.f. An ornament worn (by women) tight round the neck and hooked behind; — land ploughed four times ( = ćaus), a field four times tilled.
adj. Sixty-four
subs: s.f. The sixty-four ghaṛīs or twenty-four hours, day and night; constantly, always.

def: s.f. = ćauth, q.v.
subs: s.f. A sum paid to the zamīndār for the privilege of weighing the grain of the cultivators.

def: s.f. A four-cornered cap.
s.m. = ćabūtarā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. ćau.

ety: Prk. चउत्थो and चउत्थी; S. चतुर्थः and चतुर्थी
def: adj. Fourth ( = ćauthā); — s.f. The fourth day of a lunar fortnight (cf. ćauthī).

ety: Prk. चउत्थं, or चउत्थी; S. चतुर्थं, or चतुर्थी
def: s.f. A fourth part; a species of tribute, blackmail to the extent of one-fourth of the regular government assessment levied by the Marhaṭṭās from the neighbouring princes.
adj. (f. -ī), adj. Fourth
subs: adv. Fourthly, in the fourth place.

def: s.f. Fourth part, a quarter; a fine equal to one fourth of the revenue.
s.m. = ćauth-pan, q.v.

s.f. The fourth day; a Mohammadan ceremony observed on the fourth day after sāćak, or the day following a marriage (the bride and bridegroom visit the bride's family, and the married pair, after feasting, make a show of beating each other with sticks covered with flowers).

s.m. The receiver of a ćauth; the holder of a quarter share; the landlord's share of the produce where rents are paid in kind; — a measure in general use for grain (equal to about a ser of wheat); — a quartan ague.

ety: ćauth = ćauthā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. The fourth stage of life, old age.
adj. = ćauṅtīs, q.v.
s.f. A quartan ague ( = ćauthiyā).
s.m. A quadruped, a beast, an ox.
s.m. = چوا ćauwā, q.v.
s.m. An unguent or fragrant paste of four ingredients (viz. sandal, agallochum, saffron, and musk; or ambergris, saffron, musk, and the juice of the flowers of the Abor tristis).
adj. = چون ćawwan, ćauwan, q.v.
adj. Fifty-four.
s.m. = ćau-bār, ćaupāṛ, q.v.
adj. = ćau-bar, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi چور (cvr /⁠cor⁠/) (c. 1392), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭 (cora), from Sanskrit चोर (corá), चौर (caurá). Compare Romani chor.

def: s.m. = ćaubār, ćaupāṛ, q.v.

ety: Prk. चऊओ or चउओ = S. चतुरः
def: s.m. A measure equal to four fingers; — a kind of bamboo.
s.m. Wish, desire, longing, liking, fondness; eagerness; pleasure, taste, relish, zest, gusto; heartiness, cordiality, warmth
subs: ćāʼo-ćoz, s.m. Fondness, endearment; fondling, caressing; caress; toying, dalliance
(-ke), To become an object of regard or affection, to be a favourite, to be made much of; to have the head turned by being made much of; to take advantage of affection, &c.
To gratify a wish, satisfy a longing (of one's own, apnā).

def: s.m. Reporter, backbiter, slanderer.

ety: for ćāṅwal = S. तण्डुलं; cf. ćāćā, ćhāć, &c.
def: s.m. Rice cleared of the husk and not cooked (when boiled it is termed bhāt); — the weight of a grain of rice, one-eighth of a rattī, q.v.
subs: (-se), To make (one) chew rice (over which an incantation has been pronounced: this is done in the case of persons suspected of stealing, it being believed that the thief will be discovered by blood issuing from his mouth, or by a deficiency of saliva being produced.
wety: From Prakrit [script needed] (cāulā), and probably of Dravidian or Austroasiatic origin. Compare Punjabi ਚਾਵਲ (cāval), Punjabi ਚੌਲ (caul), Sindhi چانور (cã̄uru, “grain of rice cleansed from the husk”), Sylheti ꠌꠣꠃꠟ (saul), Bengali চাল (cal), Assamese চাউল (saul), Odia ଚାଉଳ (cāuḷa), Rohingya soil. Related to Persian چلو (čelow), which was borrowed from an Indic language.

def: s.m. = ćau-pālā, q.v.
adj. Forty-four.
s.f. = ćau-annī, q.v.s.v. ćau.
s.m. = ćauṅrī, q.v.
s.m. A report, backbiting, slander; scandal.
s.m. Land ploughed four times.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi چور (cvr /⁠cor⁠/) (c. 1392), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭 (cora), from Sanskrit चोर (corá), चौर (caurá). Compare Romani chor.

def: s.m. = ćaṅwar, and ćaur, qq.v.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi چور (cvr /⁠cor⁠/) (c. 1392), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑁄𑀭 (cora), from Sanskrit चोर (corá), चौर (caurá). Compare Romani chor.

def: s.f. A species of the pepper plant which yields long pepper, Piper ćhavya.
s.f. The plant Piper chaba.
s.m. (local), A sergeant, a beadle, a mace-bearer (syn. ćob-dār).

ety: S. चतुष्कं; or चतुर्+कः (with w inserted)
def: s.m. An aggregate of four, a four, fours, the four or quatre (at cards, dice, &c.); four-fingers' breadth, a hand-breadth; — a skein of silver thread; — pod, siliquia, or skin (of any kind of pulse, syn. ćhīmī)
subs: s.f. A gambling game played with cowries.

def: s.m. = ćavi, q.v.
s.m. Desire, &c. ( = ćāʼo, or ćāh, qq.v.).
s.m. Heap, pile, mass, collection, multitude, assemblage; a mound of earth raised to form the foundation of a building; a rampart or mound of earth raised from the ditch of a fort.

r: će, ćīm, Urdū, ća, Hindī, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, ch, cherry, jīm-ě-fārsī, jīm-ě-ʻajamī, jīm, abjad, ća-kār
def: s.m. The letter or the sound ća.
s.f. The small pox (syn. sītlā; mātā); measles (?)
subs: adj. Pitted with the small pox, pock-marked.

def: s.m. Mud, mire, puddle; ooze, slime
subs: (-kā), 'The sweat to come out (of),' to be tired, to fatigue or tire.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Muddy, miry, splashy, slimy.
adj. & s.m. Fortieth; — the fortieth day (of mourning, &c.).
v.n. To cry with pain (as a child), to pule, to moan.
s.m. = ćěhra, q.v.
s.m. Face, visage; countenance; air, mien; likeness, portrait; a mask, visor; a descr ptive roll
subs: adj. Having or bearing the king's (or queen's) head (coin)
(-kā), To have (a person's) descriptive roll made out, to enroll, to enlist; — to take service and horses
(-kā), To take down a description (of a person), to make a descriptive roll (of), to enroll, to register
s.f. Writing a descriptive roll; a descriptive roll
v.n. To be registered, be enrolled, be enlisted.

def: s.m. corr. of چہره ćěhra, q.v.
s.m. Cloth, clothes, garment, raiment.
s.m. A servant, a slave (brought up in the house); — a pupil, disciple, follower
subs: v.t. To make a disciple or convert of.

def: s.m. = ćelā, q.v.
s.f. = ćerī, ćeṭī, q.v. (and cf. the m. ćelā).
s.m.(f.?) = ćeṅd, q.v.
s.f.(?) A kind of herb which springs up in uncultivated places in the rainy season.
s.f. See s.v. ćīṅ or ćeṅ.
s.f. A needle-case ( = ćhūṅćhī).
s.m. A thin skein; — unfledged young (of a bird), a nestling
s.m. Brats, young ones; confused mass, tangle, confusion (see peć).
s.f. Denying a bet (in gaming), cheating (at play), imposture
subs: ćeṅd lānā, v.n. To practise shuffling or imposture, to cheat.

def: s.m. = ćep, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Young, little.
s.f. (dialec.), A slight cut or bruise, a scratch, a scar.
s.f. A kind of thick glossy silk, taffeta ( = P. tafta).
s.f. Stickiness, viscosity; gum, the acrid resin of fruits and trees; gum or matter (of the eye), matter, pus
subs: adj. Sticky, glutinous, viscous, &c. ( = ćipćipā, q.v.).

def: s.m. Gum, &c. ( = ćep, q.v.).; — a disease which affects standing crops.
s.f. A piece stuck on ( = ćippī, q.v.).
v.t. To stick or gum on, to paste on, to stick together ( = ćipkānā); to force (a thing) on (a person; = ćapeknā); — to pat (with the hand).
s.m. = A servant, slave, &c. ( = ćelā, q.v.).
s.m. = ćelā, ćeṭā, q.v.
s.m. Chiretta ( = ćirāʼetā, q.v.

ety: ćerā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Slavery, servitude; — discipleship.
s.f. A slave-girl ( = ćeṭī, q.v.).
s.f. = ćerī, ćeṭī, q.v.
s.m. Name of a wild tribe in the Mirzāpūr district.
s.m. A slave (see ćelā).
s.f. Action or movement of the body, motion, gesture, action, activity; striving, effort, endeavour, exertion, application, search, research; — manner, behaviour; form, figure
subs: (-kī), To strive (after), to endeavour (jāne-ko ćeshṭā karnā, 'To strive to go').

ety: S. चेतः, rt. चित्; or fr. ćhetnā, q.v.
def: s.m. Thought, perception, sensation, consciousness, wits, sense; vigilance, caution; circumspection ( = hosh), equanimity; memory, recollection, remembrance
s.m. (f. -ī, and -ikā), A servant, slave.
s.m. = ćet, and ćitt, qq.v.
s.m. Adviser, admonisher.
s.f. A wonderful deed, a miracle; magic; delusion, deception.
s f. Thought, care, anxiety; concern ( = ćintā)
s.f. Exertion, diligence; activity, readiness, expertness.
s.m. (f. -ī, and -ikā), A servant, slave.

adj. & s.m. Percipient, perceiving, observing, sentient, conscious, intelligent, rational; alive, living, feeling; of sound mind; in possession of the senses, awake, aware, wary, alert, vigilant, cautious; — a living and sentient being; — soul, spirit
subs: v.t. To make conscious, make aware; to rouse, awake; to bring (one) to (his) senses; to apprize, warn, caution
v.n. To be conscious, be wide awake, be on the alert, be wary, &c.; to come to one's senses.

def: s.m. Reason, rationality; caution, &c. ( = ćaitanya, q.v.).
s.f. Consciousness, understanding, sense, intelligence, wisdom, reflection.
s.f. A female servant or slave.

ety: ćet˚ = S. चेत(ति), or चेतय(ति), rt. चित्
def: v.t. To think of, remember; to think on, reflect on; to perceive; to advise, admonish; — v.n. To become conscious, to recover (one's) senses; to be roused, be or become aware (of); to awake, to start up; — to flare up (a light); to be blown up, blaze up (a fire)
subs: v.n. To rouse up, come to one's senses, &c.; to bethink oneself
v.n. To bethink oneself; to come to one's senses; to become conscious, to become aware (of), &c.

def: prop. چهه ćha, q.v.
adj. Six
subs: s.m. lit. 'Having six spots'; a kind of noxious insect which has six spots on its back
s.m. A denomination of money equal to six dām or two damṛī, the fourth part (properly, six twenty-fifths) of a paisā (or about sixteen cowries)
s.m. Prank, trick, cheating, jugglery (cf. tīn-pāṅć)
s.m. An angarkhā or coat, the front and back of which are made of six pieces
adj. Having six corners or angles, hexagonal
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of six months, six months old; — a boy or child six months old
adj. & s.f. Six-monthly, half-yearly, occurring once in six months; — half-yearly payment or salary; a ceremony, &c. occurring once in six months
(obl. plur. of ćha; cf. ćah-ūṅ), adj. The six, all six, the whole six.

def: s.m. The letter or sound ćha.
s.f. Beauty, grace, charm; ornament, decoration; splendour, brilliancy; — shape, form, figure, body, structure; graceful form, symmetry
subs: adj. Of delicate mould, small in shape, slender; emaciated; faded in beauty or splendour.

def: adj. Twenty-six.
s.f. = ćhab, ćhavi, qq v.
adj. (f. -ī), Handsome, comely, graceful, beautifully formed or shaped; gay, sprightly, wanton, amorous.
s.m. A large basket (made of twigs of the jhāʼu or tamarisk, or of other twigs, or of reeds; syn. ćhāwarī).
s.f. dim. of ćhabṛā, q.v.
s.m. = ćhāj, q.v.; — s.f. = ćhaćh, q.v.
s.f. Buttermilk.
adj. Dilapidated, ruined, desolate (syn. manṭhā).

ety: ćhāćh, q.v.+Prk. इल्लओ = S. aff. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with butter-milk, having or containing butter-milk.
s.f. A skimmer, a kind of spoon.
s.f. (dialec.), Butter-milk.
s.m. Lightness, airiness, frivolity, puerility; shallowness; meanness; show, display, &c.
s.f. The root of the phapond tree.
s.f. (dialec.), Boiled rice.

ety: ćhāćh, q.v.+Prk. इल्लओ = S. aff. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with butter-milk, having or containing butter-milk.
s.m. A cover, covering; a leaf; a wing.
s.m. See s.v. چهه ćha.
s.m. A water-bag.
s.m. Covering, hiding, concealing; a covering, raiment; a cover, a screen.
s.f. Thatch or roof (of a house), roof (of a carriage, &c.).
part. Covered, hidden, concealed.
s.m. A deceptive dress, disguise, masquerade; plea, pretext, pretence, trick, deceit, hypocrisy; fraud, craft, dishonesty
subs: s.m. A false ascetic, a religious hypocrite.

def: s.m. A deceptive dress, disguise, masquerade; plea, pretext, pretence, trick, deceit, hypocrisy; fraud, craft, dishonesty
subs: s.m. A false ascetic, a religious hypocrite.

def: v.n. (dialec.) = ćhidnā, and ćhandnā, qq.v.
s.f. = ćhay, ćhai, q.v.
s.m. A goat.
s.m. A goat.
adj. Coming from or relating to a goat; — s.m. (f. -ī), A goat; — s.f. A goat-skin; a bottle or vessel of leather or earth (generally having a spout).
s.m. & f. (dialec.) = ćhāgal, q.v.
s.f. A she-goat; — a kind of convolvulus, C. argenteus, or C. pes-caprae.
s.f. A she-goat; — a kind of convolvulus, C. argenteus, or C. pes-caprae.
v.t. (old H.), To taste, &c. ( = ćakhnā, q.v.).
s.f. A she-goat.
s.f. A she-goat.
s.f. A small goat, a goat ( = ćherī).
s.f. See s.v. چهه ćha.
s.f. = ćhāʼīṅ, q.v.

ety: ćhah+oṅ = Prk. ऊहं = S. नाम् (aff. of the gen. plur.)
def: adj. = ćhaʼoṅ, q.v.s.v. ćha.
s.f. Ashes.
adj. Removed; abolished; destroyed, &c.
s.f. A thatched roof (on a boat, &c.); — a stuffed pad (to prevent oxen, &c. from being galled when loaded)
subs: s.m. A pack-saddle with the pad used with it.

ety: Prk. छइल्लो and छइल्ल ओ; S. छवि+इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Handsome, comely, beautiful; spruce, foppish, gay, dashing; — a handsome fellow a gay young spark, a fop, a beau, an exquisite, a coxcomb

ety: Prk. छइल्लो and छइल्ल ओ; S. छवि+इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Handsome, comely, beautiful; spruce, foppish, gay, dashing; — a handsome fellow a gay young spark, a fop, a beau, an exquisite, a coxcomb
Spruceness, foppishness, smartness, jauntiness; gaiety, sportiveness; airs.
Spruceness, foppishness, smartness, jauntiness; gaiety, sportiveness; airs.
Spruceness, foppishness, smartness, jauntiness; gaiety, sportiveness; airs.
s.f. Shade, &c. (ćhāyā); — discoloration; a spectre, ghost.
adj. Bushy; woody, shady; — s.m. A thicket.
s.m. A kind of flat basket used in winnowing grain; a winnowing fan; — a cow-catcher; splash-board (of a vehicle)
adj. Having a long beard.
s.m. Thatching, covering in.
s.m. The expanded branches of a tree (which afford shade); a covered way; eaves (of a house); a pent (over a door-way), a portico; a veranda; a balcony; a gallery
subs: adj. Having a pent, or a portico, &c.; broad-brimmed (a turban, or hat).

ety: ćhāj˚ = Prk. छाय(इ) = S. छाट्य(ति), rt. छद्
def: v.t. To cover in (a house, &c.), to thatch.

ety: ćhāj˚ = Prk. छज्ज(इ); S. छदय(ति), rt. छद् or छन्द्
def: v.n. To be becoming (to, -ko), to become, befit, suit.
s.f. Time of milking; milking.

s.m.(f.?) Ready prepared food carried by labourers and husbandmen when they proceed to their daily work; the first meal of the day; luncheon; — large round cakes sent as a marriage present by a bride to the bridegroom.
adj. Full of liquor, drunk, intoxicated, stupefied; — ćhākṭā, s.m. A drunkard, a sot; an eater of intoxicating drugs (as opium, &c.).
adj. Full, brimful; full to repletion, crammed, satiated; full of wine, intoxicated
subs: (-ko), To fill to repletion, to satisfy, to satiate, to cram.

def: s.f. Fulness, repletion, satiety; full satisfaction; — ćhikāʼī, Settlement in full; a deed of release.
v.t. To fill to repletion, to satiate, to pamper, to cloy; to satisfy fully; — to chastise, punish
s.m. Aggregate of six; the sixth (at cards, &c.); the six or size (at dice, &c.); — a cage with a net attached to it
subs: s.m. Plots, schemes, devices, tricks; fraud, deceit, cheating
To play tricks, to practise deceit, &c.
v.n. To lose the six senses, to lose all one's wits, to be bewildered, be confounded.

def: s.m. (f.?), A thump or blow (especially on the head), a slap, a cuff, a buffet.
v.n. To be intoxicated, to be stupefied (cf. ćhāknā).

ety: prob. for ćhāṅknā; fr. S. स्यन्न (rt. स्यन्द्)+कृ; see ćhānnā
def: v.t. To clear or clean the water of a well; to strain; to purify.
v.n. To be full to repletion, to be satiated, be thoroughly satisfied; to be gratified; — to be astonished; to be harassed, be afflicted; — (dialec.) to be tricked, be deceived
s.m. A two-wheeled bullock-cart (vulg. called 'a hackery'); a cart (for carrying goods).
v.t. To thump, box, slap, cuff, buffet.
s.f. = ćhakkā, q.v.
adj. Full of liquor, drunk, intoxicated, stupefied; — ćhākṭā, s.m. A drunkard, a sot; an eater of intoxicating drugs (as opium, &c.).
s.m. Division (of crops, &c.) whereby the proprietor (or landlord) receives one-sixth.
v.t. To cause to be filled, to cause to be satiated or satisfied.
adj. = ćhail, q.v. (used in comp.)
subs: adj. & s.m. = ćhail; — s.m. A beautiful species of fragrant moss.

ety: S. छल्ली; cf. शल्कं and शल्लं
def: s.f. Skin; rind, peel; bark (cf. khāl)

s.m. Fraud, deceit, deception, knavery, imposture; sham, pretence, pretext, excuse; circumvention, trick, stratagem, subterfuge, artifice, evasion; guise, disguise, delusion, illusion, semblance, counterfeit, appearance, feint
subs: s.m. Fraud and harm, deceit, &c. ( = ćhal)
s.m. Fraud and force, trick, trickery, stratagem, &c. ( = ćhal)
vulg. ćhal-gyānī, adj. Understanding tricks or strategy, &c.
ćhal-ćhidram, s.m. Trick, plot, stratagem, &c. ( = ćhal)
ćhal-ćhidriyā, adj. Fraudulent, deceitful, tricky, false, &c.
(-ko), To deceive, trick, impose upon, to circumvent, &c. ( = ćhalnā)
v.n. To commit fraud, to practise deceit, &c.
(-ke), To be deceived or tricked (by), be imposed on (by), &c.

def: s.f. Spray; a wave, billow ( = jhāl; — in comp. it also takes the form ćhār, e.g. bāʼo-ćhār).
s.m. Skin, hide (used in comp., e.g. mr̤ig-ćhālā, 'a deer-skin'); — a blister; a pustule, pimple
subs: A blister to rise, to become blistered.

def: s.f. Running over, overflow; splashing, splash.
adj. & s.m. Deceptive, delusive; deceiving, cheating; — a deceiver, knave, cheat
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceptive, false, feigning, artful, tricky; — a deceitful or tricky person, &c.

ety: ćhalak˚ = Prk. छलक्क(इ) or छलक्के(इ), fr. S. उद्+शल्+कृ
def: v.n. To be spilt; to overflow; to spill; to splash; — v.t. To fill to the brim
subs: v.t. To fill to the brim.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Running over
s.m. Deceiving, deluding, tricking, cheating, outwitting; feigning (see ćhalnā).

ety: contrac. fr. S. उद्+शल्+लङ्घं; cf. ućhalnā
def: s.f. Skipping, jumping; skip, leap, spring, bound
subs: (or ćhalāṅgeṅ) mārnā or ćhalāng bharnā, v.n. To skip, jump, spring, &c. ( = ćhalāṅgnā).

def: s.f. = ćhalāṅg, q.v.
v.n. = ćhalāṅgnā, q.v.
v.n. To skip, jump, leap, spring, bound.
s.m. Ignis fatuus, will-o-th'-wisp
subs: v.n. 'To turn into or be like a will-o-thʼ-wisp,' to vanish or disappear suddenly, to elude search or pursuit.

def: s.f. Rustling or rippling sound; babble, murmur.

ety: fr. S. शल् or क्षल् = क्षर्, redupl.
def: v.n. To move with a rustling sound ( = sarsarānā); to make a rippling sound; to babble, to murmur; — to come off (as loose fæces) with a rumbling interrupted noise; — ćhulćhulānā, To make water slowly (as a mare, &c. in heat; cf. ćhulaknā)
subs: v.n. To move about making a rustling or rumbling noise; to flit about restlessly.

ety: ćhal(nā)+hāʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइओ (with h inserted) = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. Deceitful, tricky, &c. ( = ćhaliyā, q.v.).
s.f. A small blister, &c.
adj. & s.m. Fraudulent, deceitful, false, perfidious, treacherous; artful, tricky; — a deceiver, cheat, trickster, betrayer, &c.
s.m. A song consisting of four parts and recited with certain gestures and gesticulation.
s.f. Betel-nut.
adj. & s.m. Fraudulent, deceitful, false, perfidious, treacherous; artful, tricky; — a deceiver, cheat, trickster, betrayer, &c.
(caus. of ćhalaknā), v.t To cause to run over, to spill.
s.m. A plain ring (of gold, silver, or other metal, worn on a finger or toe)
subs: s.m. A game in which one of a party or side hides a ring in his hand, and one of an opposite party has to guess in whose hand the ring is
s.m. A ring consisting of three rings joined together, the two outer ones being plain, and the middle one having interstices
adj. Having rings; ringed, annular; — articulate; vertebrate; — having rings or spots of grease or oil on the surface (as soup, water, &c.), greasy.

def: s.m. A kind of song (usually sung by eunuchs on the occasion of the birth of a child); — name of an air or song (popular in the Panjāb).

ety: prob. a Prākr̤it form; cf. शल्कं and शल्लं
def: s.f. Skin; bark; rind, &c. ( = ćhilkā; cf. jhillī).

ety: ćhal˚ = Prk. छल(इ) or छले(इ) = S. छलय(ति), fr. the n. छल
def: v.t. To deceive, delude, trick, cheat, outwit, circumvent, impose on; to evade; to feign, pretend; to represent jalsely; to personate.
s.m. A strainer, a cloth through which anything is sifted (see ćhalnī).
s.f. An instrument for skinning or peeling.
s.f. A sieve, a strainer ( = ćhālnī, ćalnī); — adj. Battered
subs: v.n. To be pierced or perforated, to be full of holes (like a sieve); to be riddled, be battered.

def: s.f. Pardon, forgiveress, absolution; — forbearance, indulgence, favour, kindness
subs: (-ko, and -par), To pardon, forgive, excuse; to show indulgence or kindness (to).

def: s.m. An orphan, a fatherless son.
s.m. A kind of verse.
s.f. Sound of heavy rain; tinkle, jingle (of small bells, anklets, &c.), tinkle-tinkle.
v.n. To sound (as falling rain); to tinkle, jingle.
v.n. To shine, glitter, flash, &c. ( = jhamjhamānā, q.v.
v.t. To pardon, forgive.
s.f. Shade; shadow, reflection (of an object in a mirror, &c.)
s.f. A roof, a frame of bamboo for thatching, thatch; a thatched structure.

s.m. The hissing sound produced by a drop of water falling on a hot plate; — tinkle, jingle; clink, chink (of silver coin); — a bracelet (worn with ćūṛīs, the ćūṛīs being on each side and the ćhan in the middle); — gravel, grit (in grain or food; in this sense prob. fr. ćhannā).
s.m. A couplet repeated by a bridegroom at a wedding ceremony.
s.f. Straining, sifting, &c. (used in comp.)
subs: s.f. = ćhān-binān, s.m. = ćhān-bīn, s.f. Sifting, close search, scrutiny, investigation; analysis; exploration
To make close search, to sift, to scrutinize, &c.

def: s.m. An instant, a moment, the twinkling of an eye
subs: (S. kshana-vidhwaṅsī), adj. Collapsing or perishing in a moment, passing or fading away quickly, frail
adv. In an instant or moment
s.m. A moment, an instant, a little while; — adv. In a moment.

ety: ćhā˚ = Prk. छाय(इ), or छाए(इ), or छाअ(इ), or छा(इ); S. छादय(ति), rt. छद्
def: v.t. To cover; to thatch, to roof; to shade; to spread; — v.n. To spread, be spread, be diffused; to swarm; — to be shaded; — to make a home (for oneself), to take up one's abode, to abide, tarry
subs: (-par), To spread (over), to cover; to lie; to cast a shadow (on or over), to overcast, overshadow
(-ko, or -par), To cover completely, to line; to cover, &c. ( = ćhā-jānā); to fill; to be heard on all sides or all along (a road); to swarm
(-ko), To cover completely, to overspread, overcast, cloud, darken.

def: s.m. Cakes of cowdung (usually placed on the thatch or roof to dry).

ety: Prk. छणक्को and छणक्कओ; S. छन˚+क+कः
def: s.m. = ćhan; jhan, jhan-kār, qq.v.

ety: Prk. छणक्को and छणक्कओ; S. छन˚+क+कः
def: s.m. = ćhan; jhan, jhan-kār, qq.v.
v.t. = ćhanwānā, q.v.
(fr. ćhān-nā), s.f.(m.?), Chaff, husk.
